title: Green IT Energy Savings Calculator
description: Algorithm to calculate potential energy savings from server virtualization
code:
// Energy Savings from Virtualization Calculator
BEGIN
 INPUT: PhysicalServers, AvgPowerPerServer, UtilizationBeforeVirtualization
 INPUT: UtilizationAfterVirtualization, VMMOverhead, PowerForCooling

 // Calculate energy before virtualization
 EnergyBefore = PhysicalServers * AvgPowerPerServer * 8760 // hours/year
 EnergyBeforeWithCooling = EnergyBefore * (1 + PowerForCooling/100)

 // Calculate virtual servers needed
 EffectiveUtilization = UtilizationBeforeVirtualization / UtilizationAfterVirtualization
 VirtualServers = CEIL(PhysicalServers * EffectiveUtilization)

 // Calculate energy after virtualization
 EnergyAfter = VirtualServers * AvgPowerPerServer * 8760
 EnergyAfterWithCooling = EnergyAfter * (1 + (PowerForCooling - 10)/100) // 10% less cooling

 // Calculate savings
 AnnualSavings = EnergyBeforeWithCooling - EnergyAfterWithCooling
 PercentageSavings = (AnnualSavings / EnergyBeforeWithCooling) * 100

 OUTPUT: AnnualSavings, PercentageSavings
END
explanation: This algorithm calculates the energy savings achieved by consolidating physical servers into virtual machines. It accounts for the overhead of the virtualization hypervisor and reduced cooling requirements.

title: PUE Calculation Algorithm
description: Algorithm to calculate and evaluate Power Usage Effectiveness
code:
// PUE (Power Usage Effectiveness) Calculator
BEGIN
 INPUT: TotalFacilityPower, ITEquipmentPower

 // Calculate PUE
 PUE = TotalFacilityPower / ITEquipmentPower

 // Evaluate efficiency category
 IF PUE < 1.1 THEN
 Rating = "Very Efficient (Best in Class)"
 ELSE IF PUE < 1.3 THEN
 Rating = "Efficient"
 ELSE IF PUE < 1.5 THEN
 Rating = "Average"
 ELSE IF PUE < 2.0 THEN
 Rating = "Inefficient"
 ELSE
 Rating = "Very Inefficient"
 END IF

 // Calculate energy waste
 EnergyWastePercentage = ((PUE - 1) / PUE) * 100

 OUTPUT: PUE, Rating, EnergyWastePercentage
END
explanation: This algorithm calculates the PUE metric which is the industry standard for measuring data center energy efficiency. Lower PUE indicates less energy is wasted on non-computing overhead.
